T0026.title: Hardware Token
T0026.description: In banking and online payment scenarios, a 'Small Shield' (sometimes also called 'USB Shield,' 'Online Banking Shield,' 'K-Bao,' etc.) is a physical hardware device used to enhance the security of online banking transactions. It is a security medium with built-in encryption algorithms. When users perform online banking transfers, payments, or other sensitive operations, they need to generate a dynamic password or digital signature through the 'Small Shield' device to confirm the legitimacy of the transaction. In e-commerce scenarios, a 'Small Shield' is a protection mechanism that prevents script-based order sniping. When a popular product is released, a security verification mechanism is set up to prevent mass scripted purchases.
